steps.title: Express the weight as a function of volume
steps.content: The total weight $x$ equals the weight per cubic foot times the volume. Since the weight per cubic foot is 160 pounds and the volume is $V$ cubic feet: $$x = 160V$$ The volume constraint given is: $$10.4 \lt V \lt 13.4$$

steps.title: Transform the volume inequality to a weight inequality
steps.content: Multiply all parts of the volume inequality by 160 to get the weight inequality: $$160(10.4) \lt 160V \lt 160(13.4)$$ Substituting $x = 160V$: $$160(10.4) \lt x \lt 160(13.4)$$ That matches the correct answer — C is correct.
